pages.title: Currencies | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides API operations for managing currencies and foreign exchange rates in the ESG Credits solution. Users can register currencies, add FX rates for different reporting years, retrieve available currencies and rates, and remove them when no longer needed for green asset calculations.

codeExamples.description: Adds a foreign exchange rate between EUR and SEK currencies for the 2023 reporting year with a conversion rate of 10.145
Retrieves all registered currencies available in the account, returning currency codes like EUR, USD, SEK, etc.

useCases: Manage multi-currency green bond portfolios
Convert ESG investment amounts across different currencies
Set annual FX rates for consistent year-over-year reporting
Support international green asset investments
Calculate cross-border environmental impact investments

implementationSteps.title: Add Required Currencies
implementationSteps.description: Use addCurrency operation with standard 3-character currency codes (EUR, USD, SEK, etc.) that apply to your green asset universe

implementationSteps.title: Register FX Rates
implementationSteps.description: Call addFXrate with numerator/denominator currency pairs, exchange rate quota, and reporting year for accurate conversions

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve and Verify
implementationSteps.description: Use getCurrencies and getFXrates operations to list registered currencies and exchange rates for validation

Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getCurrencies Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Copy [ { "id" : 1 , "currency_code" : "EUR" } , { "id" : 2 , "currency_code" : "USD" } , { "id" : 3 , "currency_code" : "SEK" } , { "id" : 4 , "currency_code" : "DKK" } , { "id" : 5 , "currency_code" : "NOK" } , { "id" : 6 , "currency_code" : "GBP" } , { "id" : 7 , "currency_code" : "CAD" } ] Example code curl getFXrates Returns all registered foreign exchange rates POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1 Use this operation to list foreign exchange rates you have registered for a particular reporting year. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getFXrates parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Returns a list of FX rates (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type Required Description reporting_year number/4 digits yes Calendar year that the FX rate is used for Example code curl addCurrency Adds a currency to your solution account POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add currencies that you foresee being applicable to your green asset universe, and for which you want to add foreign exchange rates. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addCurrency parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200 Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Desciption currency_code string/3 char. yes Standard currency code Example code curl addFXrate Adds a foreign exchange rate between two currencies POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add foreign exchnage rate for currencies applicable to your green asset universe. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addFXrate parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description currency_code_numerator string/3 char. yes Standard currency code currency_code_denominator string/3 char. yes Standard currency code currency_code_quota number/decimal yes numerator / denominator reporting_year number/4 digits yes Calendar year that the FX rate will be used for Example code curl removeCurrency Removes a currency POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove a currency from your records Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string removeCurrency parameters * string check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description currency_code string yes Example code curl removeFXrate Removes an FX rate POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove an FX rate between two currencies Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string removeFXrate parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description currency_code_1 string yes currency_code_2 string yes reporting_year number/4 digits yes Example code curl Previous Translations Next Frameworks Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? getCurrencies Returns all registered currencies getFXrates Returns all registered foreign exchange rates addCurrency Adds a currency to your solution account addFXrate Adds a foreign exchange rate between two currencies removeCurrency Removes a currency removeFXrate Removes an FX rate Was this helpful? "denominator_currency_code": "EUR", "fx_rate": 0.13439776362121333 }, { "fx_conversion_id": 5, "year": 2022, "numerator_currency_code": "EUR", "denominator_currency_code": "NOK", "fx_rate": 10.1682 }, "denominator_currency_code": "EUR", "fx_rate": 0.09834582325288645 } ] Copy { "message": "No FX rates registered for that reporting year." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "getFXrates", "parameters": { "reporting_year": 2023 } } Copy { "message": "Currency code was successfully added." } Copy { "message": "Suggested currency code already exists." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl pages.title: Frameworks | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Details API operations for creating and managing green bond frameworks in the ESG Credits solution. Allows users to build frameworks with categories, indicators, and ESG benefits, retrieve framework details, and consolidate impact data for reporting years with multi-language support.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all private green frameworks registered for the 2023 reporting year, including framework identifiers, names, and allocated amounts

useCases: Create green bond frameworks for sustainable financing
Track use of proceeds allocation to green projects
Register ESG impact categories and indicators
Manage framework visibility across BWS accounts
Generate annual sustainability reports for stakeholders

implementationSteps.title: Create Framework
implementationSteps.description: Use addFramework operation with framework_identifier, name, currency_code, and visibility settings

implementationSteps.title: Add Year-Specific Details
implementationSteps.description: Call addYearSpecificsToFramework with value_outstanding, allocated_amount, and reporting_year parameters

implementationSteps.title: Register Impact Categories
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryIndicatorToFramework to add sub_category_id, indicator_id, and ESG benefit values

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Framework Data
implementationSteps.description: Call getFrameworkDetails with framework_identifier and reporting_year to view complete framework information

Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getFrameworks parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Copy [ { "framework_id" : 3 , "framework_identifier" : "greenbond" , "framework_name" : "Good Bank Green Bond Framework" , "framework_value_outstanding" : 22224856800 , "framework_allocated_amount" : 22224856800 , "framework_active_percentage" : 1.0 , "framework_currency_code" : "SEK" , "value_outstanding_framework" : "yes" , "framework_visibility" : "private" } ] Copy { "message" : "No such framework is registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy framework_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl getFrameworkDetails Returns details about a green framework for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operations to retrieve green framework details. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getFrameworkDetails parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (sexample) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description framework_identifier string yes String identifier for a framework reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl addFramework Creates and updates the information about a green framework POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add green frameworks to the ESG.Credits data repository. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addFramework parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description framework_identifier string yes String identifier for a framework framework_name string yes Framework name framework_currency_code string yes Currency code of the framework, as per getCurrencies framework_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl addYearSpecificsToFramework Adds reporting year specific details to a green framework POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to set up the green framework with details that are year specific. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addYearSpecificsToFramework parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description framework_identifier string yes String identifier for a framework value_outstanding number yes Amount of funding raised to green projects for the framework as a whole, for the reporting year allocated_amount number yes Amount of funding allocated to projects for the reporting year of the framework, for the corresponding impacts registered value_outstanding_framework string yes Choose "yes" when use of proceeds relate to value outstanding, choose "no" when related to allocated amount reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy Example code curl addCategoryIndicatorToFramework Adds and updates category and indicator values to a green framework POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to set up the different impact categories, indicators, and units, with specified values. pages.title: Impacts | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents API operations for calculating and retrieving environmental, social, and governance impacts from green asset investments. Supports position-level, investor-level, and portfolio-level impact reporting with optional PDF generation and multi-language output for ESG benefit certificates.

codeExamples.description: Calculates and stores the ESG impacts for a 2.4 million EUR investment over 180 days based on the green framework allocation

useCases: Calculate carbon emission reductions from green bond investments
Generate investor impact reports for ESG portfolios
Track renewable energy production benefits from investments
Consolidate portfolio-level environmental impacts
Issue ESG certificates for carbon offset credits

implementationSteps.title: Calculate Position Impact
implementationSteps.description: Use calculatePositionImpacts with position_id, investment amount, currency, term in days, and reporting year

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Impact Data
implementationSteps.description: Call getPositionImpacts with position_id and reporting_year to view calculated ESG benefits including CO2 reduction, energy production, etc.

implementationSteps.title: Generate Reports
implementationSteps.description: Use getInvestorImpacts or getPortfolioImpacts with optional report_template parameter to create PDF investor reports

Any impact data is removed when the associated position is removed. getPositionImpacts Returns the impacts of an investment in a green asset POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular position. The operation can return either a JSON formatted data set, or PDF file based on your template which you can provide to your customer as an investor report. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are stacked on each other. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getInvestorImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor asset_identifier string no For a specified asset of an investor reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl getPortfolioImpacts Returns the impacts of an investor's portfolio of investments in green assets POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular portfolio. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are consolidated when possibel (when the green framworks are the same). Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getPortfolioImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description portfolio_identfier string yes String identifier for a portfolio asset_identifier string no For a specified asset in a portfolio reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl calculatePositionImpacts Calculates and stores the impact of an investor's investment in a green asset POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a particular position already registered in ESG.Credits. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string calculatePositionImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description position_id number/integer yes Integer identifier for a position position_amount number yes Amount of money invested position_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies position_days number/integer no The term of the investment, default is 365 days reporting_year number/ 4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy Example code curl calculatePositionImpactsOnly Calculates and returns the impact of an investment in a green asset, without having to specify the investor and without storing the result POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a position without any data is stored or logged in ESG.Credits. This operation is applicable for cases when financial institutes wants to utilise ESG.Credits only for calculations and store and investor data in own data repositories. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string calculatePositionImpactsOnly parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description asset_identifier string yes String identifier for an asset position_amount number yes Amount of money invested position_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies position_days number/integer no The term of the investment, default is 365 days reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. paragraphs: This section deals with the impact from the green assets, as per the particular investments made in them. Any impact data is removed when the associated position is removed.

Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular position. The operation can return either a JSON formatted data set, or PDF file based on your template which you can provide to your customer as an investor report.
check Operation parameters
integer identifier for a investment
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Input report template name
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular investor. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are stacked on each other.
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an investor
For a specified asset of an investor
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular portfolio. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are consolidated when possibel (when the green framworks are the same).
check Operation parameters
String identifier for a portfolio
For a specified asset in a portfolio
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a particular position already registered in ESG.Credits.
calculatePositionImpacts
check Operation parameters
Integer identifier for a position
Amount of money invested
position_currency_code
Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies
The term of the investment, default is 365 days

Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a position without any data is stored or logged in ESG.Credits. This operation is applicable for cases when financial institutes wants to utilise ESG.Credits only for calculations and store and investor data in own data repositories.
calculatePositionImpactsOnly
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an asset
Amount of money invested
position_currency_code
Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies
The term of the investment, default is 365 days
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.

pages.title: Investors | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides operations for managing investor data in the ESG Credits solution, including creating investors, generating random identifiers, encrypting personally identifiable information (PII), and removing investor records. Supports secure PII handling with encryption keys for data privacy compliance.

codeExamples.description: Creates an investor record with encrypted PII including name and email using the provided encryption key
Generates a secure encryption key for encrypting and decrypting personally identifiable information in the ESG Credits repository

useCases: Register green bond investors with encrypted personal data
Generate anonymous investor identifiers for privacy
Manage multi-currency investor portfolios
Retrieve investor lists with decrypted information
Comply with data privacy regulations for ESG reporting

implementationSteps.title: Generate Encryption Key
implementationSteps.description: Call generateEncryptionKey operation to create a secure key for PII encryption and store it safely

implementationSteps.title: Create Investor Identifier
implementationSteps.description: Use generateInvestorIdentifier to create random non-PII identifiers, or use your own identifiers

implementationSteps.title: Add Investor
implementationSteps.description: Call addInvestor with investor_identifier, name, currency_code, email, and encryption_key to register the investor with encrypted PII

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API Taxonomy Translations Currencies Frameworks Projects Issuers Assets Investors Positions Portfolios Impacts Blockchain Users X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits ESG Credits API Investors This section has operations to manage investors, i.e. showing, adding, encrypting, and removing investors getInvestors Returns all investors POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all investors. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getInvestors parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Copy [ { "investor_id" : 19 , "investor_identifier" : "3a6515809856" , "investor_name" : "John Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } , { "investor_id" : 20 , "investor_identifier" : "9c809eb86ee6" , "investor_name" : "Mary Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } ] Copy { "message" : "No investors are registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description depryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to Example code curl generateInvestorIdentifier Generates a random investor identifier POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate investor identifiers, for cases where they need to random and not personally identifiable information (PII). Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateInvestorIdentifier Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl generateEncryptionKey Generates an encryption key used to encrypt personal identifiable information POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate an encryption, used to encrypt and decrypt personally identiable infomation (PII) in the data repository of ESG.Credits. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateEncryptionKey Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl addInvestor Creates and updates the information of an existing investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create investors stored in ESG.Credits. Is also used to alter already existing investor data. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addInvestor parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor, potentially personally identifiable information (subject to encryption) investor_name string yes Personally identiable information (subject to encryption) investor_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investor, as per getCurrencies (currently not utilized) investor_email string no For investor correspondence e.g investor reports encryption_key string no For encrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl removeInvestor Removes an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove investors from the ESG.Credits data repository. pages.title: Issuers | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents operations for managing green asset issuers in the ESG Credits solution. Users can add issuers of green bonds or sustainable assets, set visibility (public/private), retrieve issuer lists, and remove issuers when no longer needed for portfolio management.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates an issuer record for Good Bank with EUR currency and private visibility across BWS accounts

useCases: Register banks issuing green bonds
Manage corporate issuers of sustainable assets
Track multiple issuers in green investment portfolios
Set issuer visibility for shared or private data
Remove issuers no longer offering green assets

implementationSteps.title: Add Issuer
implementationSteps.description: Use addIssuer operation with issuer_identifier, issuer_name, currency_code, and visibility (public/private) parameters

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Issuers
implementationSteps.description: Call getIssuers with issuer_visibility parameter to list public or private issuers in your account

implementationSteps.title: Remove Issuer
implementationSteps.description: Use removeIssuer with issuer_identifier when the issuer is no longer active (requires no associated assets)

Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getIssuers parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error messagee Copy [ { "issuer_id" : 1 , "issuer_identifier" : "35f0c1a1c08" , "issuer_name" : "X Bank" , "issuer_currency_code" : "EUR" , "issuer_visibility" : "public" } , { "issuer_id" : 6 , "issuer_identifier" : "good-bank-issuer" , "issuer_name" : "Good Bank" , "issuer_currency_code" : "SEK" , "issuer_visibility" : "private" } ] Copy { "message" : "No such issuer is registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add issuers of green assets. This operation also alters already existing issuer information. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer issuer_name string yes Issuer name issuer_currency_code string yes Currency code of the asset, as per getCurrencies issuer_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl removeIssuer Removes an issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove issuers of green assets from the ESG.Credits data repository. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string removeIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer Example code curl Previous Projects Next Assets Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? getIssuers Returns private or public issuers addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer removeIssuer Removes an issuer Was this helpful? Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "getIssuers", "parameters": { "issuer_visibility": "public" } } Copy { "message": "Issuer was successfully added." } Copy { "message": "Invalid issuer visibility term used." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "addIssuer", "parameters": { "issuer_identifier": "good_issuer", "issuer_name": "Good Bank", "issuer_currency_code": "EUR", "issuer_visibility": "private" } } Copy { "Issuer was successfully removed." } Copy { "message": "Issuer was not removed. Asset(s) exist." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "removeIssuer", "parameters": { "issuer_identifier": "good_issuer" }

pages.title: Portfolios | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides API operations for managing investor portfolios of green assets. Users can create portfolios, add positions to portfolios, retrieve portfolio compositions, remove positions, and consolidate ESG impacts at the portfolio level for comprehensive sustainability reporting.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new portfolio named 'portfolio_green_john' for investor with identifier 9c809eb86ee6
Associates investment position ID 19 with the specified portfolio for consolidated impact tracking

useCases: Create segregated green investment portfolios for investors
Group multiple green assets for consolidated reporting
Track portfolio-level ESG impacts across investments
Manage investor-specific sustainable asset allocations
Generate portfolio impact reports for stakeholders

implementationSteps.title: Create Portfolio
implementationSteps.description: Use addPortfolio operation with investor_identifier and portfolio_identifier to establish a new portfolio container

implementationSteps.title: Add Positions
implementationSteps.description: Call addPositionToPortfolio with portfolio_identifier and position_id to associate investments with the portfolio

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Portfolio Data
implementationSteps.description: Use getPortfolios or getPortfolioPositions to view portfolio composition and all associated green investments

Default is all. user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl getPortfolioPositions Returns all investments in green assets in a portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all green investments in an investors portfolio. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getPortfolioPositions parameters * JSON check Operation Parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation Parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description portfolio_identifier string yes String identifier for a portfolio Example code curl addPortfolio Creates and updates the information of an existing portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create a portfolio for an investor, next to be associated with specific investments. This operation is also used to alter already existing portfolio information. Impacts can then be consolidated on portfolio level. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addPositionToPortfolio parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identfier string yes String identifier for an investor portfolio_identifier string yes String identifier for a portfolio position_id number/integer yes Integer identifier for an investment Example code curl removePositionFromPortfolio Removes an investment in a green asset from a portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove positions from a portfolio, prior to removal of the investment as such. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string removePortfolio parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor portfolio_identifier string yes String identifier for a portfolio Example code curl Previous Positions Next Impacts Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? getPortfolios Returns all portfolios of all investors getPortfolioPositions Returns all investments in green assets in a portfolio of an investor addPortfolio Creates and updates the information of an existing portfolio of an investor addPositionToPortfolio Adds an investment in a green asset to a portfolio of an investor removePositionFromPortfolio Removes an investment in a green asset from a portfolio of an investor removePortfolio Removes a portfolio of an investor Was this helpful? pages.title: Positions | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents operations for managing investment positions in green assets. Users can create positions linking investors to assets, specify investment amounts and terms, retrieve position details, calculate ESG impacts, and remove positions when investments are closed or redeemed.

codeExamples.description: Creates an investment position of 2.5 million EUR in asset XS123456 for 180 days starting February 1, 2023

useCases: Register investor purchases of green bonds
Track individual green asset holdings
Calculate position-specific ESG impacts
Manage investment terms and start dates
Update position amounts as investments change

implementationSteps.title: Create Position
implementationSteps.description: Use addPosition with investor_identifier, asset_identifier, amount, currency, reporting_year, days, and start_date parameters

implementationSteps.title: Calculate Impact
implementationSteps.description: The system automatically calculates ESG impacts if reporting_year is provided, or call calculatePositionImpacts separately

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Positions
implementationSteps.description: Call getPositions with investor_identifier and optional asset/issuer filters to view all investments

For decrypting personally identifiable information
Use this operation to register investments to a particular investor.
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an investor
String identifier for an asset
Amount of money invested
position_currency_code
Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies
Year of the taxonomy (if impact calculation is to be done at this stage)
The term of the investment, default is 365 days (if impact calculation is to be done at this stage)
Date of investment (for ESG Credits certificate generation)
Use this operation to remove an investment from a particular investor, where also any associated ESG impact records will be removed.
check Operation Parameters
String identifier for an investor
Integer identfier for an investment

pages.title: Projects | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Details operations for managing green projects and building frameworks from project consolidation. Users can register projects with impact characteristics, add yearly details, associate projects with frameworks, consolidate multiple projects into frameworks, and manage project lifecycle with multi-language support.

codeExamples.description: Creates a green project for a wind park associated with a green bond framework with private visibility

useCases: Register renewable energy projects for green bonds
Track use of proceeds allocation to specific projects
Build frameworks by consolidating multiple projects
Manage project-level ESG impact indicators
Report on individual green project benefits

implementationSteps.title: Create Project
implementationSteps.description: Use addProject with project_identifier, name, currency_code, framework_identifier, and visibility parameters

implementationSteps.title: Add Project Details
implementationSteps.description: Call addYearSpecificsToProject with allocated_amount and reporting_year, then use addCategoryIndicatorToProject for impact data

implementationSteps.title: Build Framework
implementationSteps.description: Use buildFrameworkFromProjects with framework_identifier to consolidate all associated projects into a complete framework

pages.title: Taxonomy | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents taxonomy building operations including categories, sub-categories, indicators, units, UN Sustainable Development Goals, and EU Environmental Objectives. Enables users to construct custom ESG taxonomies with multi-language support for comprehensive sustainability classification and reporting frameworks.

codeExamples.description: Adds a renewable energy category to the 2023 taxonomy in English language

useCases: Build custom ESG classification taxonomies
Align green frameworks with UN SDGs
Map projects to EU Environmental Objectives
Define impact indicators and measurement units
Create multi-year taxonomy structures for reporting

implementationSteps.title: Define Categories
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategory operation to create top-level ESG categories like renewable energy, circular economy, etc.

implementationSteps.title: Add Sub-Categories and Indicators
implementationSteps.description: Call addSubCategory with category_id, then add units and indicators to measure specific impacts

implementationSteps.title: Link SDGs and Objectives
implementationSteps.description: Use addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory to associate UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives with taxonomy elements

Default is the original language. Example code curl addCategory Adds and updates a category to the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add a category to your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addCategory parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description category_name string yes Category name to add or change reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy category_name_changed string no New category name language_code string yes Selection of original language code as per getLanguages Example code curl addSubCategory Adds and updates a sub category to the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add a sub category to your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addSubCategory parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description sub_category_name string yes Sub category name to add or change category_id number/integer yes Integer identifier for a category reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy sub_category_name_changed string no New sub category name language_code string yes Selection of original language code as per getLanguages Example code curl addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory Adds a SDG and EU Objective to a sub category for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives to a sub category of your taxonomy. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory parameters * string check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description sub_category_id number, integer yes Integer identifier for a sub category reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy sdg_id number/integer no Integer identifier for an SDG eu_objective_id number/integer no Integer identifier for an EU Environmental Objective Example code curl addUnit Adds and updates a unit to the taxonomy for a particular reporting year POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add a unit to your taxonomy. pages.title: Translations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Translations API for ESG Credits, enabling multilingual support for taxonomies and frameworks. Provides operations to manage language translations for categories, sub-categories, super-indicators, indicators, and units across different reporting years.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all available languages for registering translations in ESG taxonomy
Adds or updates a Swedish language translation for a category in the 2023 taxonomy
Adds a Swedish translation for an indicator in the 2022 reporting year

useCases: Create multilingual ESG reporting frameworks for international investors
Translate environmental impact categories into multiple European languages
Localize sustainability indicators for regional compliance requirements
Provide language-specific taxonomy for global green bond issuers
Enable cross-border ESG impact reporting in native languages

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Available Languages
implementationSteps.description: Call getLanguages operation to obtain the list of supported language codes (en, sv, dk, nb, fi, de, pl, fr, nl, es, pt, it) for your translations

implementationSteps.title: Add Category Translations
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryTranslation, addSubCategoryTranslation, addSuperIndicatorTranslation, and addIndicatorTranslation operations to register translations for each taxonomy element with reporting_year and language_code parameters

implementationSteps.title: Register Unit Translations
implementationSteps.description: Call addUnitTranslation for measurement units, specifying unit_id, reporting_year, language_code, and the translated phrase even if units are identical across languages

pages.title: Users | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Users API for ESG Credits, managing end users, groups, and investor access permissions. Provides operations to create, update, and remove users, assign them to groups (bankers, investors, capitalists, etc.), and control their access to specific investor data.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new user with encrypted personal information and assigns a user role
Assigns a user to the bankers group for role-based access control
Grants a user access to view specific investor information and portfolios

useCases: Manage banking advisors and their access to client ESG portfolios
Control institutional investor access to green asset impact data
Assign venture capitalists to specific project sponsor information
Create segregated user groups for issuers, agents, and regulators
Implement role-based access control for ESG reporting platforms

implementationSteps.title: Create Users
implementationSteps.description: Use addUser operation with user_identifier, name, email, role (user or admin), and optional encryption_key for secure data storage

implementationSteps.title: Assign Group Membership
implementationSteps.description: Call getGroups to retrieve available groups (bankers, investors, capitalists, issuers, agents, exchanges, regulators), then use addGroupToUser to assign users to appropriate groups

implementationSteps.title: Configure Investor Access
implementationSteps.description: Use addInvestorToUser to grant users access to specific investor portfolios and impact data, enabling segregated data access control

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides an overview of the ESG Credits solution for financial institutions. Explains how the API enables rapid development of ESG impact reporting for green investments, supporting digital channels and investor reporting with customizable taxonomies and blockchain certification.
useCases: Banks creating investor reports for green assets in digital channels
Institutional investors viewing portfolio-level ESG impact reports
Venture capitalists providing sponsors with consolidated project impact data
Green asset issuers publishing impact details for promotional purposes
Certification bodies digitally publishing quality-assured green frameworks
Preventing double-counting of green project benefits across debt capital markets

implementationSteps.title: Define Your Taxonomy
implementationSteps.description: Create custom or ICMA-based taxonomy with two levels of impact categories and indicators, linking to UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives as needed

implementationSteps.title: Register Green Frameworks and Projects
implementationSteps.description: Input impact profiles for green frameworks and projects, supporting multiple currencies and languages for international reporting

implementationSteps.title: Generate Impact Reports
implementationSteps.description: Calculate per annum or term-based impact amounts for individual investments or consolidated portfolios, with optional blockchain certification for immutability

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits Solution Overview BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include: Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market. Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets. Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix. Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations. Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets. By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting. Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit. Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework. You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable. The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community. paragraphs: BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview

This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include:
Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market.
Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets.
Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix.
Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets.

By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting.

Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit.

Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework.

You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable.

The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community.

pages.title: Calendar | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Calendar API for FAN Game Cube, representing scheduled games, matches, or tournament instances. Provides operations to create, list, and delete calendars that anchor the gameplay timeline for season-based, game-based, or tournament-based fan campaigns.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new calendar for a sports season or tournament, returning a calendarId for linking matches
Retrieves a specific calendar or lists all calendars created for organizing game schedules
Response after successfully creating a calendar, providing the unique identifier

useCases: Create single-event calendars for UEFA Champions League finals
Manage entire season calendars for LaLiga 2025-2026
Schedule multiple matches across different tournaments
Organize weekend chess tournaments with specific time frames
Set up yearly cricket season schedules for fan engagement

implementationSteps.title: Create Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Call new_calendar operation with a name parameter (e.g., 'LaLiga 2025-26') to create a time-bound event container

implementationSteps.title: Store Calendar ID
implementationSteps.description: Save the returned calendarId from the API response to use when creating related Field objects and scheduling matches

implementationSteps.title: Link to Matches
implementationSteps.description: Use the calendarId to associate specific games, matches, or tournament instances with this calendar timeline

The Calendar object anchors the gameplay timeline, enabling season-based, game-based, or tournament-based fan campaigns. Examples: UEFA Champions League Final : Madrid vs PSG, for a single event calendar. LaLiga 2025-2026 , including multiple matches, for the entire season calendar. new_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Calendar name (e.g. LaLiga 2025-26) new_calendar API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "new_calendar" , "parameters" : { "name" : "2025-26" } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); new_calendar Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the calendarId , which will be used to link with related objects. list_calendars POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters list_calendars Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string Calendar Id list_calendars API Call Example list_calendars API call using Node.js list_calendars Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of calendars you created or a single calendar if the CalendarId parameter is included in the call. delete_calendar POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption calendarId string Then Calendar Id of the calendar to delete. delete_calendar API Call Example delete_calendar API call using Node.js delete_calendar Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Cubes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Cubes API for FAN Game Cube, representing zones or tiles within a Field. Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by fans, tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches with automatic generation when creating fields.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves a paginated list of cubes for a specific field with their coordinates and ownership status
Updates the price for multiple cubes identified by their i_j coordinates to 500 cents (5 USD)
Response showing successful cube price updates and any invalid cube identifiers

useCases: Sell NFT field zones to fans for specific matches or seasons
Track cube ownership across multiple games and tournaments
Set dynamic pricing for premium field locations near goals or center field
Distribute rewards to cube owners when events occur in their zones
Enable secondary market trading of field cube NFTs

implementationSteps.title: List Available Cubes
implementationSteps.description: Use list_cubes operation with fieldId and limit parameters to retrieve cubes, using lastEvaluatedKey for pagination if the field has hundreds of cubes

implementationSteps.title: Set Cube Pricing
implementationSteps.description: Call update_cube-price with fieldId, cubesList (using i_j coordinate format), and priceInCents to establish pricing for specific zones

implementationSteps.title: Track Ownership and Events
implementationSteps.description: Monitor cube ownership status and link live events to cubes using their x, y coordinates for reward distribution

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Cubes Represents a zone or tile within a Field . Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field. list_cubes POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_cubes parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. limit number The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes). lastEvaluatedKey (optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes. list_cubes API Call Example list_cubes API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "list_cubes" , "parameters" : { "fieldId" : "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" , "limit" : 5 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); list_cubes Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns a list of cubes and their related cube data, including the cubeId . If more cubes are available, the field lastEvaluatedKey is returned (include it on the API call for getting the next chunk of cubes). update_cube-price POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_cube-price parameters JSON check method parameters update_cube-price Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id cubesList list A list of cubes whose price needs to be updated. Use cube data i and j indexes to identify each cube using the format i_j (e.g. 40_0). priceInCents number Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price) update_cube-price API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js update_cube-price Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the number of correctly updated cubes, the number of invalid detected cubes, and the list of invalid cubes. pages.title: Event Types | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Event Types API for FAN Game Cube, defining in-game occurrences like goals, assists, or plays. Event types are registered with point values and affect cube owners whose zones match the action location during live matches.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new event type called 'pass' worth 100 points for a specific field

Set higher point values for critical game events like winning goals
Create sport-specific event types for cricket, basketball, or hockey
Adjust reward economics by updating event type point values

implementationSteps.title: Create Event Types
implementationSteps.description: Use new_event-type operation to define game occurrences with eventTypeName and eventTypePoints for a specific fieldId

implementationSteps.title: Configure Point Values
implementationSteps.description: Set appropriate point values for each event type based on game significance (e.g., 100 for passes, 1000 for goals)

implementationSteps.title: Link to Live Events
implementationSteps.description: Use the returned eventTypeId when registering live events to automatically calculate cube owner rewards based on spatial coordinates

pages.title: Field | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Field API for FAN Game Cube, representing virtual sports fields tokenized into interactive zones. Fields serve as the foundational canvas for game interactions, linked to real-world sports venues with dimensions specified in centimeters.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new football field representing Camp Nou with 120x80 meter dimensions divided into 3x3 meter cubes
Retrieves field details including name, sport, and dimensions for a specific field or all fields
Response after successfully creating a field, providing the unique identifier for linking cubes and matches

useCases: Create tokenized versions of famous stadiums like Camp Nou and Real Madrid Stadium
Set up basketball courts for Madison Square Garden fan engagement
Define cricket field dimensions for yearly season tournaments
Configure chess board grids for weekend tournaments
Establish virtual sports venues for multiple sports on one platform

implementationSteps.title: Define Field Parameters
implementationSteps.description: Specify name, sport type, fieldLength and fieldWidth in centimeters, and cubeLength for the square size of individual zones (e.g., 300 for 3x3 meter cubes)

implementationSteps.title: Create Field
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field operation which automatically generates all cubes for the field based on dimensions and cube size

implementationSteps.title: Use Field ID
implementationSteps.description: Store the returned fieldId to link calendars, matches, events, and manage the generated cubes throughout the game lifecycle

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Field Represents a virtual sports field tokenized into interactive zones or sections ("CUBES"). It is the foundational canvas for all game interactions. Each field is typically linked to a real-world sports match. Examples: Real Madrid Stadium Madison Square Garden new_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_field parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Field name (e.g. Camp Nou) sport string Sport (e.g. Football) fieldLength number Field length in centimeters (e.g. 12000) fieldWidth number Field width in centimeters (e.g. 9000) cubeLength number The square size of a single cube (e.g. 300 for a cube of 3x3 meters) new_field API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js new_field Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the fieldId , which will be used to link NFT Game Cube related objects. All the cubes for that field are created when creating a new field. list_fields POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters list_fields Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id list_fields API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js list_fields Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of fields you created or a single field if fieldId parameter is included in the call. delete_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_field parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id to delete. delete_field API Call Example delete_field API call using Node.js delete_field Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. All the cubes created and assigned to the deleted field are also deleted. pages.title: Live Events | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Live Events API for FAN Game Cube, recording in-match events at specific coordinates during scheduled matches. Events are captured manually via dashboard, automatically through AI models, or programmatically via application integration for real-time fan engagement scoring.

codeExamples.description: Registers a live event at x:45, y:40 coordinates during a match, triggering cube-based reward calculations
Successful response after recording a live event for processing cube owner rewards

useCases: Capture goals, assists, and fouls during live soccer matches
Record chess moves automatically using AI move detection
Track basketball shots and passes at specific court coordinates
Register cricket ball trajectories for fan engagement rewards
Integrate third-party sports data feeds for automated event capture
Manual event entry by match organizers via dashboard interface

implementationSteps.title: Configure Event Integration
implementationSteps.description: Choose integration method: manual dashboard entry, BWS AI models for automatic detection, or custom application API calls

implementationSteps.title: Register Live Events
implementationSteps.description: Call new_event operation with matchId, fieldId, calendarId, event coordinates (x, y), and eventTypeId during matches

implementationSteps.title: Process Rewards
implementationSteps.description: System automatically maps event coordinates to corresponding cubes and distributes points to cube owners based on event type point values

It is the primary mechanism by which game data—such as goals, moves, fouls, or plays—is captured and mapped to corresponding Cubes for scoring and reward processing. This API serves as a trusted interface to power fan engagement: Manually via the organizer dashboard or solution UI (BWS Solution Interface) Automatically via BWS-provided machine learning models that detect and push game events in real time (e.g., AI-driven goal recognition, chess move capture) Through your application. new_event POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_event parameters * JSON check method parameters new_event Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id for which you want to register the event. fieldId string The Field Id the match is linked to. calendarId string The calendar Id the match is linked to. event JSON check event parameter new_event Event Parameter Parameter Type Desciption coordinates JSON check coordinates parameter eventTypeId string The Event Type Id of the event you want to register. new_event Coordinates Parameter Parameter Type Desciption x string The 2D x coordinate of the event. y string The 2D y coordinate of the event. new_event API Call Example new_event API call using Node.js new_event Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Match | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Match objects in FAN Game Cube, which represent time-bound gameplay sessions tied to specific Fields. Covers creating, updating, listing, and deleting matches with team information, scheduling, and image uploads via IPFS integration.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new match with team information, scheduling, and image upload for PSG vs Man City
Lists matches for a specific calendar, optionally filtering by matchId
Deletes a specific match from the calendar

useCases: Schedule football matches like PSG vs Manchester City with team flags and images
Create basketball game sessions for NBA teams like Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears
Schedule chess tournament rounds between players like Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura
Track match status (scheduled, playing, finished, canceled) for live events
Set pricing for NFT cube purchases tied to specific matches

implementationSteps.title: Create a new match
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match operation with calendarId, startTimeInMillis, team names, flags, and match images (base64 encoded) to create a gameplay session

implementationSteps.title: Update match details
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match with matchId to modify match information like status, description, or pricing as the event progresses

implementationSteps.title: List and manage matches
implementationSteps.description: Use list_matches to retrieve all matches for a calendar or specific match details, and delete_match to remove completed or canceled matches

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Match A Match is a time-bound, gameplay session tied to a specific Field, during which events are tracked, points are awarded, and fan interactions occur. It represents a game instance—such as a football match, chess round, or tournament heat—scheduled via the Calendar object and hosted on a defined Field .\ Examples: PSG vs. Manchester City Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. new_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match parameters * JSON check method parameters new_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and startTimeInMillis). Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match should be included in. startTimeInMillis * number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled". priceInCents number The default price in USD cents for all the cubes if no specific price is defined for a cube (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD). new_match API Call Example new_match API call using Node.js new_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the newly created match. update_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_match parameters * JSON check method parameters update_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and matchId) Only provide those you want to update. Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match is included in. matchId * string The Match Id we want to update. startTimeInMillis number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled" priceInCents number The price in USD cents (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD) update_match API Call Example update_match API call using Node.js update_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the updated match. list_matches POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_matches parameters JSON check method parameters list_matches Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. matchId string (optional) The Match Id we want to list. list_matches API Call Example list_matches API call using Node.js list_matches Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matches for the provided calendar. If we give a Match Id, it will just return the match we want to list. delete_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_match Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id of the match we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. delete_match API Call Example delete_match API call using Node.js delete_match Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. Previous Plays (Field-Calendar) Next Prizes Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? new_match Request Body new_match API Call Example update_match Request Body update_match API Call Example list_matches Request Body list_matches API Call Example delete_match Request Body delete_match API Call Example Was this helpful? pages.title: Plays (Field-Calendar) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Field-Calendar relationships in FAN Game Cube, which model the temporal lifecycle of a Field. Allows linking static spatial configurations (like sports grounds) to dynamic calendar entries representing tournaments, seasons, or individual matches.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new field-calendar relationship for PSG 2025-26 season with image upload to IPFS
Lists all calendar entries linked to a specific field
Deletes a field-calendar relationship

useCases: Create PSG Champions League 2026 tournament calendar for a football field
Setup New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season calendar for basketball court
Link multiple tournament seasons to a single sports field configuration
Manage temporal lifecycle of field usage across different events
Track historical seasons and tournaments for specific sports venues

implementationSteps.title: Link field to calendar
implementationSteps.description: Use new_field-calendar with fieldId and calendarId to create a relationship between static field configuration and dynamic tournament/season calendar

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve calendar associations
implementationSteps.description: Use list_field-calendars with fieldId to view all calendar entries linked to a specific sports field

implementationSteps.title: Remove outdated relationships
implementationSteps.description: Use delete_field-calendar to remove completed seasons or canceled tournaments from field associations
