pages.title: Prizes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Prize objects in FAN Game Cube, which represent rewards for cube owners based on match or season performance. Supports creating prizes with positions (1st, 2nd, 3rd), descriptions, multiple images, and HTML content for enhanced presentation.

codeExamples.description: Creates a first place prize for a match with name, description, and snapshot image
Lists all prizes configured for a specific match
Updates prize HTML description for enhanced presentation

useCases: Create signed merchandise prizes for top performing cube owners
Offer VIP match tickets as rewards for first place winners
Configure Zoom calls or AMAs with athletes as exclusive prizes
Distribute bonus BWS tokens to top three leaderboard positions
Setup digital NFT prizes for match or season performance

implementationSteps.title: Create match prizes
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match-prize to define up to three prizes (positions 1, 2, 3) with names, descriptions, and snapshot images for each match

implementationSteps.title: Enhance prize presentation
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match-prize to add HTML descriptions and additional images (image1, image2, image3) for detailed prize information

implementationSteps.title: Manage prize catalog
implementationSteps.description: Use list_match-prizes to view all configured prizes and delete_match-prize to remove outdated or canceled rewards

pages.title: FAN Game Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive overview of FAN Game Cube platform explaining how sports organizations can tokenize field sections as NFT cubes for fan engagement. Covers multi-sport scalability, pricing strategies, revenue models, one-click purchase widgets, and real-time fan engagement mechanics across football, chess, basketball, cricket, and other sports.
useCases: Tokenize football stadium sections into 500-2000 cubes for match engagement
Create chess tournament engagement by tokenizing 64 chessboard squares
Divide basketball courts into zones (three-point arc, paint zone) for fan participation
Tokenize cricket pitch batting zones and boundary sections for match interaction
Enable secondary marketplace trading of cubes with royalties for sports clubs
Launch full-season FAN Game Cube events for NBA or NFL teams

implementationSteps.title: Define cube allocation
implementationSteps.description: Determine number of cubes (500-2000) and field division strategy based on sport type, ensuring premium areas like penalty boxes or center court have strategic value

implementationSteps.title: Set pricing strategy
implementationSteps.description: Configure dynamic pricing with higher rates for prime field areas and lower rates ($10 or less) for secondary zones to encourage broad participation

implementationSteps.title: Integrate purchase widget
implementationSteps.description: Add one-click JavaScript widget to club website for frictionless credit card purchases, enabling fans to buy cubes as easily as game tickets

implementationSteps.title: Enable real-time engagement
implementationSteps.description: Provide fans access to mobile app for managing cube collections, tracking live matches, and competing on leaderboards for prizes

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview Building New Revenue Streams for the Sports Industry BWS's FAN Game Cube platform revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own digital assets linked to specific sections of a sports field. This extends beyond football to include chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, and any sport with a field that can be split into "cubes". As real-time events unfold in their selected area, fans earn points and unlock exciting rewards, all while creating new revenue opportunities for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and other key figures in the sports industry. Want to launch a FAN Game Cube event? Contact us here . Cube Allocation for Different Sports Sports organizers can define how many cubes to sell and how to divide their playing area based on the sport’s unique characteristics. A football/soccer field cubes division. Examples include: Football stadiums It is typically divided into 500, 1000, or 2000 cubes , ensuring key areas like penalty boxes and midfield zones hold premium value. Chess tournaments The 64 chessboard squares can be tokenized, offering fans a unique way to interact with their favorite chess events. Tennis courts Tokenizing the baseline, service boxes, and net area allows fans to engage in real-time match events. Cricket fields Dividing the pitch into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections enhances fan participation. Basketball courts Cube allocation can include key areas like the three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone . Rugby fields Fans can purchase cubes covering try zones, scrums, and midfield sections . Baseball fields Key locations such as bases, outfield zones, and pitcher’s mound can be tokenized. And a long etcetera. NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading FAN Game Cube utilizes NFT technology to ensure verifiable ownership. However, the usage of NFT goes beyond ownership, allowing fans to trade their cubes as they would with traditional assets. During a season or tournament, cube owners can buy and sell cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces, generating additional royalties for sports clubs and organizers. Since NFTs offer permanent and transparent ownership records , fans gain long-term value while clubs benefit from secondary sales. Pricing and Monetization Strategy Organizers have complete control over pricing, ensuring dynamic valuation based on strategic cube placement. Different pricing strategies include: Prime field areas Higher prices can be charged for high-demand sections, such as the center of a football pitch, penalty spots, or key cricket fielding positions. Secondary zones To encourage broad participation, less strategic locations, such as side areas or backfield positions, can be offered at $10 or lower. Dynamic pricing models Pricing can fluctuate based on match importance, demand, and historical engagement levels. Revenue Potential Your expected revenue is calculated as follows: Number of cubes × Average Price per cube = Total income For example, launching 500 cubes at an average price of $500 per cube on a VIP event can generate $250,000 in revenue. Larger stadiums, premium tournaments, or full-season FAN Game Cubes can scale this model to generate even higher revenues. Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget BWS provides a one-click purchase widget for frictionless transactions. Sports clubs do not need to create special accounts or navigate complex payment gateways—cubes can be sold in just a few hours. A weekend Chess Tournament NFT Game Cube event. The widget integrates easily into existing club websites, allowing fans to purchase cubes as simply as buying a regular game ticket. How Fans Can Participate and Win Once a sports club publishes its one-click buy widget , fans can easily purchase FAN Game Cubes using standard credit cards, making participation straightforward and accessible. One-click FAN Game Cube purchase experience This experience brings a new layer of engagement to the fan experience while giving them direct ownership of in-game digital assets. Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics NFT-based cubes offer fans an engaging way to experience their favorite sports, providing: True ownership of digital sports assets. Fans can buy, sell, or hold tradeable assets for future value (full-season FAN Game Cube). Passive earnings potential through resale royalties and long-term value accumulation. Access to real-time engagement through a dedicated mobile app. Once a cube is purchased, fans can: Manage their cube collection across multiple sports. Track upcoming matches in real time. Join live leaderboards to compete with other fans for top prizes. Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Multi-Sport Scalability Works across various sports, including football, cricket, chess, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, golf, Formula 1, and esports. It is flexible and adaptable to different event structures and fan bases. One-Click Buy Widget A JavaScript widget for effortless integration that enhances the club’s purchasing experience with quick, seamless transactions. Easy & Free to Launch Clubs and organizers can set up Fan Game Cube events using a simple three-step workflow or API calls. Flexible Event Calendars Supports single VIP games, full-season matches, and tournaments. It is scalable, from small local events like the Ohio Chess Tournament to major leagues like NBA teams. Real-Time Fan Engagement A comprehensive mobile app enables clubs to foster deep connections with fans during matches. Features include live-streaming for real-time game viewing and interactive chat to enhance fan engagement and discussion. BWS Fan Game Cube is the future of sports engagement, bridging the digital and real-world experiences for fans while unlocking new revenue opportunities for sports organizations. Get started today and transform the way fans interact with their favorite games! Previous FAN Game Cube Next FAN Game Cube API Last updated 27 days ago Was this helpful? Cube Allocation for Different Sports NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading Pricing and Monetization Strategy Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget How Fans Can Participate and Win Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Coordinate X engagement raids to amplify post visibility
Generate automated analytics reports for tracked accounts

Basic Commands (4 commands) Essential bot operations including help, initialization, and status checks. Commands: /help - Displays available bot commands and their descriptions. /start - Bot initialization command that handles different workflows based on context and user state. /status - Checks the bot's operational status and provides basic information about bot availability. /get_chatid - Returns the chat ID of the group where the command is executed. Advanced Filtering (3 commands) Create named filters for complex tracking scenarios and multi-project management. Commands: /set_x_filtering - Sets up monitoring filters for X (Twitter) content that will be tracked and included in reports. /get_x_filtering - Retrieves and displays all currently configured X (Twitter) monitoring filters for the group. /delete_x_filtering - Deletes specific X (Twitter) monitoring filters or all filters for the group. Project Configuration (10 commands) Customize project metadata including name, description, logo, and URLs for reports. Commands: /set_project_name - Sets the project name for the community. /get_project_name - Retrieves and displays the current project name. /set_project_description - Sets the project description for the community. /get_project_description - Retrieves and displays the current project description. /set_project_long_description - Sets the detailed project description for the community. /get_project_long_description - Retrieves the current detailed project description. /set_project_logo - Initiates the project logo upload flow. /get_project_logo - Retrieves and displays the current project logo URL. /set_project_urls - Sets project URLs (website, social media, documentation, etc. /get_project_urls - Retrieves the current project URLs. Raid Commands (11 commands) Coordinate X (Twitter) engagement raids to amplify post visibility and reach. Commands: /raidx - Starts a new raid on an X (Twitter) post. /raid_status - Check current raid progress. /raid_stop - Stops an active raid before completion. /raid_history - Show past raids. /set_raid_defaults - Configure default raid settings. /get_raid_defaults - View current raid settings. /set_raid_message_behavior - Configure how raid status messages are updated (edit/repost/pin). /get_raid_message_behavior - View current raid message update behavior. /set_raid_image - Initiates the raid image upload flow. /auto_raid_config - Configure automatic raid for best tweet of the day. /get_auto_raid_config - View current auto-raid settings. Schedule Management (3 commands) Configure automated report generation schedules with custom intervals. Commands: /set_schedule - Sets a schedule for when reports should be automatically generated. /get_schedule - Retrieves and displays the current scheduled time for automatic report generation. /delete_schedule - Removes the configured daily schedule for automatic report generation. Calendar & Time (2 commands) Manage calendar settings and time periods for report generation cycles. Commands: /set_calendar - Configures the start date and cadence (period) for report generation. /get_calendar - Retrieves and displays the current calendar configuration including start date and cadence. Reports (2 commands) Generate and manage performance analytics reports for tracked X content. Commands: /report - Displays the last generated report for the group. /recreate - Forces the generation of a new report by triggering the tweet fetching and report generation. Leaderboard Customization (10 commands) Customize scoring points, colors, titles, and display settings for leaderboard reports. Commands: /set_points - Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_points - Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics. /set_colors - Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_colors - Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics. /set_top_title - Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. /set_best_title - Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. /set_engagement_title - Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. /get_titles - Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including. /set_top_count - Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. /get_top_count - Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report. Topic Management (2 commands) Configure Telegram topic/thread settings for bot operation. Commands: /set_topic - Sets the specific topic (forum thread) where the bot should send reports in Telegram groups. /get_topic - Retrieves and displays the currently configured topic (forum thread) where the bot. Admin (1 commands) Administrative functions for managing bot notifications and permissions. Commands: /add_admin - Designates specific group administrators to receive private bot notifications (e. Subscription & Credits (5 commands) Manage billing, subscriptions, credits, and usage tracking. Commands: /subscription - Provides access to Stripe customer portal for subscription management including billing history,. /buy - Initiates the purchase process for X Bot Professional subscription. /get_credits - Displays the current credit usage and availability for the bot. /payment_success - Handles successful payment confirmation after Stripe checkout completion. /payment_cancelled - Handles payment cancellation when users exit Stripe checkout without completing payment. pages.title: Admin | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for single administrative command /add_admin that designates specific group administrators to receive private bot notifications about credit exhaustion and errors. Supports adding up to 10 designated admins or clearing configuration to notify all group administrators.

codeExamples.description: Designate specific administrators to receive private bot notifications
Remove all designated admins and revert to notifying all group administrators

useCases: Designate specific admins to receive credit exhaustion notifications
Configure error notification recipients for bot issues
Limit notification spam by selecting key administrators
Clear admin configuration to notify all group admins by default

implementationSteps.title: Designate notification recipients
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_admin @user1 @user2 command to specify up to 10 administrators who should receive private bot notifications

implementationSteps.title: Clear admin configuration
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_admin clear to remove designated admins list and revert to notifying all group administrators

pages.title: Advanced Filtering | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for three advanced filtering commands that create named filters for complex tracking scenarios. Includes /set_x_filtering for creating filters with X API query syntax, /get_x_filtering for viewing configurations, and /delete_x_filtering for removing filters with wildcard support.

codeExamples.code: /set_x_filtering my_kols_1 from:@CryptoHayes OR from:@IncomeSharks keywords:BWS mention:@BWSCommunity
codeExamples.description: Create named filter for tracking specific accounts with keywords and mentions
Create filter tracking project mentions and hashtags
Delete specific filter named 'my_kols_1'
Delete all filtering queries using wildcard

useCases: Track multiple KOL accounts with complex OR conditions
Monitor project mentions across hashtags and direct mentions
Create trending topic filters combining multiple keywords
Setup multi-project tracking with named filter configurations
Remove outdated filters or clear all tracking configurations

implementationSteps.title: Create named filter
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_x_filtering with unique filter name and X API query syntax supporting from:, mention:, keywords:, OR, AND, hashtags and mentions

implementationSteps.title: View filter configuration
implementationSteps.description: Use /get_x_filtering to retrieve and display all configured filters with their query syntax

implementationSteps.title: Delete filters
implementationSteps.description: Use /delete_x_filtering with filter name to remove specific filter, or use wildcard '*' to clear all filters

paragraphs: Sets up monitoring filters for X (Twitter) content that will be tracked and included in reports. Supports multiple filter types including user mentions, keywords, and account filtering. Each filter is saved with a unique name and can contain complex query syntax. Only group administrators can execute this command.
Includes automatic handling of X API query length limits (512 characters)
Persists configuration in the database for future use
filter_name (required): Unique identifier for this filter configuration
filtering_query (required): X search query syntax using supported operators
Supported operators: from:, mention:, keywords:, OR, AND, hashtags (#), mentions (@)
Complex queries with multiple conditions are supported

Retrieves and displays all currently configured X (Twitter) monitoring filters for the group. Shows filter names and their associated query syntax, helping users understand what content is being tracked for report generation.

Deletes specific X (Twitter) monitoring filters or all filters for the group. Allows administrators to remove individual filters by name or clear all filters using the wildcard "*" parameter. Only group administrators can execute this command.
filter_identifier (required):
Specific filter name to delete individual filter
"*" wildcard to delete all filters

pages.title: Filter Setup | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents filter setup commands for X Bot, including viewing, adding, removing, and clearing filters for accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, excludes, and ignore lists. All commands update the X API query filter automatically with 512-character limit handling. Admin-only commands require group administrator permissions.

codeExamples.description: Lists all filters configured for the current chat
Add X/Twitter accounts to the default filter for tracking
Add keywords to the filter configuration for content tracking

useCases: Track specific Twitter accounts for community engagement monitoring
Filter tweets by keywords relevant to crypto projects
Monitor cashtags like $BTC or $ETH for token discussions
Exclude unwanted terms from analytics reports
Manage mention tracking for brand monitoring

implementationSteps.title: View Current Filters
implementationSteps.description: Use /list_filters or /show_filter to check existing filter configuration and verify current tracking settings

implementationSteps.title: Add Tracking Criteria
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_accounts, /add_keywords, /add_cashtags, or /add_mentions commands to specify what content to track from X/Twitter

implementationSteps.title: Configure Exclusions
implementationSteps.description: Use /add_excludes or /add_ignore to filter out unwanted content from reports

implementationSteps.title: Manage Filters
implementationSteps.description: Use /remove_ or /clear_ commands to adjust or reset filter configurations as needed

pages.title: Leaderboard Customization | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Details leaderboard customization commands for X Bot reports, including setting scoring multipliers for engagement metrics, configuring HTML colors for visual display, customizing section titles, and controlling the number of users shown. All settings persist in the database and require administrator permissions to modify.

codeExamples.description: Sets scoring multipliers for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and views to calculate leaderboard points
Configures HTML hex colors for displaying engagement metrics in leaderboard reports
Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports

useCases: Customize point values to prioritize replies over likes for engagement scoring
Apply brand colors to leaderboard reports for consistent visual identity
Configure custom titles for different report sections to match community language
Adjust leaderboard display count to show top 20 performers instead of default 10
Set zero points for views to exclude them from scoring calculations

implementationSteps.title: Configure Scoring System
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_points with five numeric values to define how much each engagement type (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, views) contributes to leaderboard scores

implementationSteps.title: Customize Visual Appearance
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_colors to define HTML hex colors for each metric, and /set_top_title, /set_best_title, /set_engagement_title to customize section headings

implementationSteps.title: Set Display Preferences
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_top_count to control how many top users appear in the leaderboard report

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Commands Leaderboard Customization /set_points Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. These multipliers determine how many points each type of engagement contributes to a user's total score. Only group administrators can execute this command. Persists configuration in the database for future use Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Copy /set_points 5 1 20 10 0.01 Parameters: likes (required): Points awarded per like (positive number, can be decimal) retweets (required): Points awarded per retweet (positive number, can be decimal) replies (required): Points awarded per reply (positive number, can be decimal) quotes (required): Points awarded per quote (positive number, can be decimal) views (required): Points awarded per view (positive number, can be decimal) Validation: Each value must match positive number format (regex: /^[0-9]+(.[0-9]+)?$/) /get_points Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. Shows either configured values or indicates default values are being used. Persists configuration in the database for future use Usage: Parameters: None required /set_colors Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) in the leaderboard reports. Colors are stored as hexadecimal values and used for report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command. Persists configuration in the database for future use Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Parameters: likes (required): HTML hex color for likes display (e.g., #FF0000 or #F00) retweets (required): HTML hex color for retweets display (e.g., #00FF00) replies (required): HTML hex color for replies display (e.g., #0000FF) quotes (required): HTML hex color for quotes display (e.g., #FFFF00) views (required): HTML hex color for views display (e.g., #FF00FF) Validation: Each color must match HTML hex format (#RRGGBB or #RGB) /get_colors Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard reports. Shows either configured colors or default values if none are set. Persists configuration in the database for future use Usage: Parameters: None required /set_top_title Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. Allows customization of the primary heading displayed at the top of leaderboard reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Persists configuration in the database for future use Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Parameters: title_text (required): Text content for the title (in quotes if contains spaces) color (required): Hex color code in #RRGGBB format (e.g., #476a30) /set_best_title Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above the highlighted best tweet. Only group administrators can execute this command. Persists configuration in the database for future use Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Parameters: title_text (required): Text content for the title (in quotes if contains spaces) color (required): Hex color code in #RRGGBB format (e.g., #ff6b6b) /set_engagement_title Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above engagement scoring rules and metrics. Only group administrators can execute this command. Persists configuration in the database for future use Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Parameters: title_text (required): Text content for the title (in quotes if contains spaces) color (required): Hex color code in #RRGGBB format (e.g., #28a745) /get_titles Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including top title (main leaderboard), best title (best tweet), and engagement title (scoring rules). Shows either configured values or default values for each section. Persists configuration in the database for future use Usage: Parameters: None required /set_top_count Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. This controls how many top-performing users are shown in the generated reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Persists configuration in the database for future use Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Parameters: count (required): Positive integer representing number of users to show in leaderboard Validation: Must be a positive integer (regex: /^[1-9][0-9]*$/) /get_top_count Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report leaderboard. Shows either the configured value or indicates default value (10) is being used. Persists configuration in the database for future use Usage: Parameters: None required Related Pages Reports - Generate and manage performance analytics reports for tracked X content. Project Configuration - Customize project metadata including name, description, logo, and URLs for reports. pages.title: Project Configuration | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Covers project configuration commands for X Bot, including setting project name, description, long description, logo upload, and project URLs. These settings are displayed in reports and on the xbot.ninja website. All configuration commands require administrator permissions and persist in the database.

codeExamples.description: Sets the project name that will be displayed in reports and on the website

codeExamples.code: /set_project_description "A revolutionary DeFi platform for community governance"
codeExamples.description: Configures a short project description providing context about the project's goals
Sets multiple project URLs separated by spaces for website, social media, and documentation links

useCases: Configure project metadata for display on xbot.ninja discovery platform
Brand analytics reports with project name and logo
Provide project description for investor discovery
Add social media and website links for community reference
Upload custom logo for professional report appearance

implementationSteps.title: Set Basic Information
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_project_name and /set_project_description to configure your project's basic identification and purpose

implementationSteps.title: Upload Visual Branding
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_project_logo command, then send an image file that will be uploaded to S3 for public access in reports

implementationSteps.title: Add Project Links
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_project_urls with space or comma-separated URLs to add website, social media, and documentation links

pages.title: Raid Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents raid commands for coordinating community engagement campaigns on X/Twitter posts. Includes starting raids with target goals, tracking progress, stopping raids, viewing history, and configuring default settings and automatic raids. Features auto-mute options and customizable engagement targets for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and bookmarks.

codeExamples.description: Starts a new raid on an X/Twitter post using default settings
Starts a raid with custom target goals, 1-hour duration, and auto-mute enabled

codeExamples.code: /set_raid_defaults likes=N retweets=N replies=N quotes=N bookmarks=N duration=30m mute=yes/no enabled=yes/no
codeExamples.description: Configures default raid settings for target goals, duration, and auto-mute behavior

useCases: Coordinate community engagement campaigns on important project announcements
Track progress toward engagement goals with real-time status updates
Run timed raids with automatic chat muting until targets are met
Configure automatic raids on the best tweet of the day from reports
Manage raid history to analyze past campaign performance

implementationSteps.title: Configure Default Settings
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_raid_defaults to establish baseline target goals, duration, and mute preferences for all future raids

implementationSteps.title: Start a Raid
implementationSteps.description: Use /raidx with an X/Twitter post URL and optional custom parameters to launch an engagement campaign

implementationSteps.title: Monitor Progress
implementationSteps.description: Use /raid_status to check current progress toward engagement targets during active raids

implementationSteps.title: Enable Auto-Raids
implementationSteps.description: Use /auto_raid_config to automatically launch raids on top-performing tweets from daily reports

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Commands Raid Commands /raidx Starts a new raid on an X (Twitter) post. Allows community members to engage with the post (likes, retweets, replies, quotes) and tracks progress toward target goals. Only group administrators can start raids. Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 likes=50 retweets=20 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 duration=1h mute=yes Parameters: <post_url> (required): Full X/Twitter post URL likes=<number> (optional): Target number of likes (default from settings) retweets=<number> (optional): Target number of retweets (default from settings) replies=<number> (optional): Target number of replies (default from settings) quotes=<number> (optional): Target number of quotes (default from settings) bookmarks=<number> (optional): Target number of bookmarks (default from settings) duration=<time> (optional): Raid duration (default from settings) - Format: 30m, 2h, 1d mute=<yes|no> (optional): Auto-mute chat until targets met (default: no) /raid_status Check current raid progress Manages raid lifecycle and status tracking Usage: /raid_stop Stops an active raid before completion Manages raid lifecycle and status tracking Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: /raid_history Show past raids Usage: /set_raid_defaults Configure default raid settings Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Parameters: likes=N - Default target likes retweets=N - Default target retweets replies=N - Default target replies quotes=N - Default target quotes bookmarks=N - Default target bookmarks duration=30m - Default duration (e.g. 30m, 2h, 1d) mute=yes/no - Default auto-mute enabled=yes/no - Enable/disable raids /get_raid_defaults View current raid settings Usage: /set_raid_message_behavior Configure how raid status messages are updated (edit/repost/pin) Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: /get_raid_message_behavior View current raid message update behavior Usage: /set_raid_image Initiates the raid image upload flow. After executing this command, the bot will wait for the user to send an image file which will be saved as the raid image. The image is uploaded to the website S3 bucket for public access and displayed in raid announcement messages. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: /auto_raid_config Configure automatic raid for best tweet of the day Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Parameters: enabled=yes/no - Enable/disable auto-raid duration=360 - Raid duration in minutes (default: 360 = 6 hours) delay=5 - Delay before raid starts after report (default: 5 minutes) likes=N - Target likes (default: 100) retweets=N - Target retweets (default: 50) replies=N - Target replies (default: 20) quotes=N - Target quotes (default: 10) bookmarks=N - Target bookmarks (default: 50) mute=yes/no - Auto-mute chat during raid (default: no) /get_auto_raid_config View current auto-raid settings Usage: Related Pages Reports - Generate and manage performance analytics reports for tracked X content. Admin - Administrative functions for managing bot notifications and permissions. pages.title: Reports | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Explains report generation commands for X Bot. The /report command displays the last generated report with user engagement statistics and rankings. The /recreate command forces new report generation, consuming credits and optionally deleting existing history. Both commands show leaderboard data based on configured scoring rules.

codeExamples.description: Displays the most recent leaderboard report including image and text components with user engagement statistics
Forces generation of a new report by triggering tweet fetching process, consuming credits
Generates a new report and deletes all existing tweets and reports from S3 storage

useCases: View the latest community engagement leaderboard
Generate fresh analytics after configuring new filters
Create on-demand reports for community competitions
Reset analytics history when starting a new tracking period
Verify tracking configuration with immediate report generation

implementationSteps.title: View Existing Report
implementationSteps.description: Use /report to retrieve and display the most recently generated leaderboard report with engagement statistics

implementationSteps.title: Generate New Report
implementationSteps.description: Use /recreate to force immediate report generation, fetching latest tweets and calculating new rankings

implementationSteps.title: Clear History (Optional)
implementationSteps.description: Add delete-history flag to /recreate command to remove all existing report data from storage before generating new report

Retrieves and sends the most recent leaderboard report including both image and text components. The report shows user engagement statistics and rankings based on configured scoring rules. Usage: Copy /report Parameters: None required /recreate Forces the generation of a new report by triggering the tweet fetching and report generation process. This command consumes credits and can optionally delete existing report history. Only group administrators can execute this command, and requires X token and filtering queries to be configured. paragraphs: Displays the last generated report for the group. Retrieves and sends the most recent leaderboard report including both image and text components. The report shows user engagement statistics and rankings based on configured scoring rules.

Forces the generation of a new report by triggering the tweet fetching and report generation process. This command consumes credits and can optionally delete existing report history. Only group administrators can execute this command, and requires X token and filtering queries to be configured.
Persists configuration in the database for future use

pages.title: Schedule Management | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents schedule management commands for automating report generation. Supports both simple daily time scheduling in HH:MM format and advanced AWS EventBridge cron expressions for complex schedules. Commands allow setting, viewing, and deleting scheduled report generation with day-of-week support and UTC timezone.

codeExamples.description: Generates reports daily at 12:30 UTC using simple time format
Generates reports on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday at 14:30 UTC
Uses AWS EventBridge cron expression to generate reports at 12:00 UTC on weekdays only

useCases: Automate daily leaderboard reports at a consistent time
Schedule reports for specific days when community is most active
Configure complex schedules using cron expressions for advanced timing
Set reports to generate during team standup meetings
Align report timing with community events or competitions

implementationSteps.title: Set Schedule
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_schedule with HH:MM format for daily reports, or add day names/numbers for specific days, or use cron expression for complex patterns

implementationSteps.title: Verify Configuration
implementationSteps.description: Use /get_schedule to display the current scheduled time and confirm it's set correctly in UTC timezone

implementationSteps.title: Remove Schedule
implementationSteps.description: Use /delete_schedule to remove the configured schedule and stop automatic report generation

Supports both simple daily time scheduling (HH:MM format) and advanced cron expressions for complex schedules. Creates an EventBridge rule for scheduled report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Copy /set_schedule 12:30` - Generate reports daily at 12:30 UTC Copy /set_schedule 14:30 Mon Wed Fri` - Generate reports on Monday, Wednesday, Friday at 14:30 UTC Copy /set_schedule 09:00 1 7` - Generate reports on Monday (1) and Sunday (7) at 09:00 UTC Copy /set_schedule 16:00 Monday Wednesday` - Generate reports on Monday and Wednesday at 16:00 UTC Copy /set_schedule cron(0 12 ? * MON-FRI *)` - Generate reports at 12:00 UTC on weekdays only Copy /set_schedule cron(0 9 ? * MON *)` - Generate reports every Monday at 09:00 UTC Parameters: schedule (required): Either: Time in HH:MM format (24-hour, UTC timezone) for daily schedules Time and days: HH:MM followed by day names/numbers (e.g. "14:30 Mon Wed Fri") AWS EventBridge cron expression for complex schedules Validation: HH:MM format with valid hours (00-23) and minutes (00-59) Day formats: Numbers (Monday=1-7), short names (Mon-Sun), long names (Monday-Sunday) Valid AWS EventBridge cron syntax: cron(Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week Year) /get_schedule Retrieves and displays the current scheduled time for automatic report generation. Shows the configured UTC time or indicates if no schedule is set. Usage: Parameters: None required /delete_schedule Removes the configured daily schedule for automatic report generation. Deletes the EventBridge rule and database entry. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only: Yes - Only group administrators can execute this command. Usage: Parameters: None required Related Pages Calendar & Time - Manage calendar settings and time periods for report generation cycles. Reports - Generate and manage performance analytics reports for tracked X content. Advanced Filtering - Create named filters for complex tracking scenarios and multi-project management. pages.title: Subscription & Credits | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Covers subscription and credit management commands for X Bot. Includes accessing the Stripe customer portal for subscription management, initiating PRO subscription purchases, checking credit usage, and handling payment success/cancellation callbacks. FREE tier shows remaining credits while PRO shows unlimited usage.

codeExamples.description: Opens Stripe customer portal for managing subscription, billing history, payment methods, and cancellation
Initiates the purchase process for X Bot Professional subscription with Stripe checkout integration
Displays current credit usage and availability, showing remaining credits for FREE tier or unlimited for PRO

Upgrade to PRO subscription for unlimited report generation
Manage billing information and payment methods through Stripe portal

Cancel or modify subscription plan

pages.title: Topic Management | BWS API Docs

The /set_topic command configures where the bot sends reports within a specific forum thread, and must be executed in the target thread. The /get_topic command retrieves the currently configured topic for reports.

Retrieves and displays the currently configured topic thread where the bot sends reports

Route analytics updates to specific community sections
Manage multiple bot functions across different forum topics
Verify report destination configuration in forum-enabled groups

The setup process differs based on your use case — choose the path that matches your needs. Prerequisites Before installing X Bot, ensure you have: A Telegram account Admin access to a Telegram group (or ability to create one) An X (Twitter) account you want to track Basic familiarity with Telegram bots and group management Choose Your Installation Path Select the setup guide that matches your use case: Crypto Project Setup : Track your project's X engagement and community activity Individual KOL Setup : Monitor your personal X performance metrics KOL Team Setup : Track multiple team members' performance in one place Crypto Project Setup Goal: Track your project's X engagement and community activity (optionally use for competitions, prizes, or airdrops) 1 Find and Add the Bot Search for @BWS_X_Bot in Telegram Add the bot to your project's main Telegram group Make the bot an admin with the following settings: Admin rights required: Yes Special permissions needed: None 2 Create Your X Analytics Query The following is a simple example for first-time installation. You can customize tracking later. Track your official X account: Track your project's cashtag: Example: What this does: Tracks all posts from your project's X account Monitors posts mentioning your cashtag (e.g., $SOL ) Collects engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Includes tracked items in leaderboard rankings 3 Schedule Daily Reports Set up automatic daily analytics reports: What this does: Generates automated reports daily at 12:00 UTC Posts performance screenshots and leaderboards to your group Tracks top accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Keeps community engaged with regular updates Tip: Choose a time when your community is most active for maximum engagement. 4 Verify Installation Force an initial report to verify everything is working: What this does: Creates an immediate performance report based on your configured tracking Shows leaderboards for accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Confirms tracking is working correctly Gives you a preview of daily automated reports 5 Add Project Metadata (Optional) Add project information to be featured on the xbot.ninja website: What this does: Displays your project on the xbot.ninja website for investor and community discovery Helps investors find projects with authentic community traction Showcases your project's performance metrics publicly 6 Customize Your Reports (Optional) Personalize reports with custom branding. Set Custom Point Weights (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Bookmarks, Impressions): Set Custom Colors: Set Custom Titles: Individual KOL Setup Goal: Get publicly listed on xbot.ninja to showcase your competencies and performance metrics. 1 Create Your Analytics Group Open Telegram and create a new group Name it something like "My X Analytics" or "KOL Performance Tracker" This can be a private group for personal tracking Tip: Keep this group private or share it with potential clients to showcase your metrics. 2 Find and Add X Bot Search for @BWS_X_Bot in Telegram Add the bot to your newly created group Make the bot an admin with: Admin rights required: Yes Special permissions needed: None 3 Track Your X Account Monitor your personal X account performance: Example: What this does: Tracks all your posts and engagement metrics Builds a verifiable portfolio of your performance Enables discovery on xbot.ninja Provides data for client reports 4 Add Your Topics Track keywords relevant to your content: What this does: Shows which topics you're covering Demonstrates your niche expertise Tracks community engagement with your topics Helps projects find you based on relevant keywords 5 Enable Regular Reports Get daily performance insights: What this does: Sends daily performance reports at 09:00 UTC Tracks engagement trends over time Helps identify top-performing content Provides data for client reporting 6 Create Your First Report Generate your first analytics report: What this does: Creates an immediate snapshot of your performance Shows engagement metrics and rankings Verifies tracking is working correctly Gives you shareable proof of your influence 7 Add Your Profile Metadata (Optional) Add profile information to be featured on the xbot.ninja website: What this does: Displays your KOL profile on xbot.ninja Helps project owners discover and hire you Showcases engagement metrics and expertise publicly To get publicly listed: Maintain a minimum performance threshold by posting and engaging consistently Build authentic engagement (avoid vanity metrics) Monitor daily reports to track progress toward listing Note: Featured KOLs are continuously monitored to maintain quality standards. KOL Team Setup Goal: Track team performance accurately and show clients real, measurable results 1 Create Team Analytics Group Open Telegram and create a new group Name it "KOL Team Analytics" or "[Team Name] Performance Tracker" This will be your shared analytics dashboard Tip: Make this group private and invite only team members and trusted clients. 2 Find and Add Bot as Admin Search for @BWS_X_Bot in Telegram Add the bot to your team group Make the bot an admin: Admin rights required: Yes Special permissions needed: None 3 Add Team Members Invite all KOL team members to the group: Click the group name to open group info Tap "Add Members" Select all team members Ensure everyone can see the analytics and reports 4 Track All Team Accounts Add all team members' X accounts for tracking: Example: What this does: Tracks team members' posts and engagement Creates team-wide leaderboards Enables performance comparisons Provides aggregate metrics for client reports 5 Set Projects You Work For Monitor client projects with cashtags: Example: What this does: Tracks team engagement with client tokens Shows which members promote which projects Demonstrates ROI to clients with verifiable metrics Identifies top performers per campaign 6 Schedule Team Reports Set up daily team performance reports: What this does: Generates daily reports at 10:00 UTC Posts leaderboards showing top team members Tracks trends for each project Provides regular updates for team and clients Tip: Schedule for team standups or planning meetings. 7 Create Your First Report Generate your first team analytics report: What this does: Creates an immediate team performance snapshot Shows individual and aggregate metrics Verifies all accounts are tracked correctly Provides shareable data for client presentations 8 Add Team Metadata (Optional) Feature your team on xbot.ninja : What this does: Displays your KOL team on xbot.ninja Helps project owners discover and hire your team Showcases team engagement metrics publicly 9 Customize Team Branding (Optional) Personalize reports with custom titles: 10 Exclude Internal Accounts (Optional) Hide internal/test accounts from leaderboards: Verification and Testing 1 Check bot status The bot should appear as an admin in your group 2 Test a command Run /help to see all available commands 3 Generate a report Run /recreate to create your first analytics report 4 Review the output Ensure accounts and keywords are tracked correctly 5 Check scheduled reports Wait for your scheduled time to verify automation Common Issues and Solutions Bot Not Responding Possible causes: Bot not made admin in the group Bot was removed or banned Network connectivity issues Solutions: Verify bot has admin status Remove and re-add the bot Check Telegram server status No Data in Reports Possible causes: Accounts or keywords not configured Tracking period too short (wait a few hours) X accounts have privacy restrictions Solutions: Run /get_accounts and /get_keywords to verify configuration Wait for X API to collect data (can take a few hours) Ensure tracked X accounts are public Reports Not Generating Possible causes: No schedule set Schedule time in wrong timezone (should be UTC) Insufficient data for report generation Solutions: Run /get_schedule to verify configuration Set schedule with /set_schedule HH:MM (UTC time) Manually generate report with /recreate to test Installation takes just a few minutes. Once configured, X Bot runs automatically, providing scheduled analytics to help you track, measure, and improve your X engagement. pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands Getting Support Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions X Bot X Bot Overview What is X Bot? X Bot is a Telegram-based analytics platform that provides trusted X (Twitter) analytics for crypto communities. It enables tracking, analysis, and reporting of X activity directly within Telegram groups. X analytics you can trust X Bot uses the official X API to ensure accuracy and reliability. We tested several cheaper alternatives and even built our own "posts crawler," but none matched the accuracy of the official API. Many third-party analytics tools you find online show up to 50% discrepancies in their metrics. With X Bot, you get data directly from the source you can trust. The Problem X Bot Solves In the crypto space, it's challenging to: Get a trusted measure of community engagement and the real impact of hired KOLs' profitability Identify projects with genuine community traction vs. artificial engagement Find and hire KOL teams that deliver actual results Track and measure authentic X (Twitter) engagement Verify influencer performance with transparent metrics Discover legitimate investment opportunities backed by real community activity X Bot solves these problems by providing verifiable X analytics that distinguish authentic engagement from vanity metrics. Key Features Analytics & Tracking Track X (Twitter) accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Monitor engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Generate automated scheduled reports with performance insights (up to 12-hour frequency) View leaderboards ranking top performers by engagement Performance Scoring X Bot uses a scoring algorithm that weighs different engagement types based on their value. The weights are fully configurable to match each project's specific needs and priorities. Example scoring formula (weights can be customized): Example Engagement Weights Explained: Likes (×1): Basic engagement, shows content appreciation Retweets (×2): Amplification - user shares content paragraphs: X Bot is a Telegram-based analytics platform that provides trusted X (Twitter) analytics for crypto communities. It enables tracking, analysis, and reporting of X activity directly within Telegram groups.
In the crypto space, it's challenging to:
Get a trusted measure of community engagement and the real impact of hired KOLs' profitability
Identify projects with genuine community traction vs. artificial engagement
Find and hire KOL teams that deliver actual results
Track and measure authentic X (Twitter) engagement
Verify influencer performance with transparent metrics
Discover legitimate investment opportunities backed by real community activity
X Bot solves these problems by providing verifiable X analytics that distinguish authentic engagement from vanity metrics.
Track X (Twitter) accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions
Monitor engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions)
Generate automated scheduled reports with performance insights (up to 12-hour frequency)
View leaderboards ranking top performers by engagement
Example scoring formula (weights can be customized):
Example Engagement Weights Explained:
Likes (×1): Basic engagement, shows content appreciation
Retweets (×2): Amplification - user shares content with their followers
Replies (×1.5): Active conversation starter
Quotes (×3): High-value engagement - adds commentary while sharing
Bookmarks (×2): Content worth saving for later reference
Impressions (×0.001): Reach metric - how many people saw the post
For a post with: 50 likes, 10 retweets, 8 replies, 3 quotes, 15 bookmarks, 5,000 impressions

Product Blurb Product Blurb Target: 100-200 words | Actual: 155 words, 1037 chars Copy X Bot - Accurate X Analytics You Can Trust 🔍📊 X Bot is the ultimate Telegram-based analytics platform that gives you accurate X (Twitter) engagement metrics straight from the official X API. 📈 With X Bot, crypto projects can measure authentic community impact and hired KOL profitability, KOL teams can showcase verifiable performance to clients, and investors can discover legitimate projects with real community traction. X Bot's powerful features include tracking X accounts, keywords, cashtags and mentions, monitoring key engagement metrics, generating automated reports, and ranking top performers with customizable scoring. 🏆 You can personalize your reports with branded colors and titles, and even get featured on xbot.ninja to be discovered by projects and investors. Whether you're a crypto project boosting community engagement, an individual KOL building a proven track record, or a KOL team demonstrating ROI to clients, X Bot provides the transparent, trusted analytics you need to succeed in the crypto space. 📣🚀 Target Audiences Crypto Projects - Crypto projects need X Bot to track community engagement, run competitions, and build authentic participation around their project and token. Individual KOLs - Individual Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) need X Bot to showcase their X performance metrics and get discovered by projects seeking influencers. KOL Teams - KOL teams and agencies need X Bot to track their collective performance across multiple X accounts and demonstrate ROI to their clients with verifiable data. Test blockchain solutions for free on Mumbai or Sepolia networks
Monitor monthly blockchain credit usage and costs
Budget for blockchain transactions with transparent fee structure
Run up to 1000 testnet transactions for free with subscription

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees Fees Calculator API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Platform Fees We take care of everything. Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”. Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized. Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience. Fees Calculator Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience. In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use. If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks. You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free. We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged. Using test networks does not consume $BWS tokens. Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details, You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free. When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit. Every solution requires $BWS tokens to execute and fund the developer's community and platform maintenance. Please read the platform White Paper to know more about the $BWS token and its tokenomics. Pricing Example Suppose you subscribe to Blockchain Web Services and want to create 100 new NFTs on the Polygon blockchain using BWS.NFT.zK to create the NFTs, and BWS.IPFS.Upload to save your images to IPFS. Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you: Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan. How do I pay those Fees? To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage. BLOCKCHAIN CREDIT FEE We use the Blockchain Credit Fee as the minimum billable unit, 0.01 USD. If, for example, you spend 2 USD creating NFTs, we will add 200 blockchain credit fee units to your next bill. Credits Usage balance. Previous Quick Start Next API How Tos Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Fees Calculator Pricing Example How do I pay those Fees? Was this helpful? Copy 100 IPFS Images, included = 0 USD 100 NFTs x 0.02 Network Fee = 2 USD

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Quick Start Writing "Hello World!" to the blockchain in just one call! Get your API key Your API requests should be authenticated using your API key (any request that doesn't include an API key will return an error). Learn how to Get your API Key . Your First BWS API Request We're going to use the BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution to save a message to the blockchain. Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call Let's write "Hello World!" to the Matchain blockchain network without managing network fees or Web3 wallets by calling a regular Web2 API. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Hash version * number 1 network * string matchain operation * string insert parameters * JSON { key: "mykey", value: "Hello World!" } Please copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your API Key). curl Javascript Phyton If executed correctly, check for jobId in the response body part. We will use it to verify that the blockchain operation has been completed correctly and to receive the blockchain transaction receipt and Certificate of Trust . API Call response Check for Status To check for job completion and results, use the jobId you got from the previous call. Please note that interacting with blockchain(s) is asynchronous, so it may take some time for the operation to finish and for the blockchain receipt to be received. Fetch for the blockchain job status and results. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/fetch Using the previous call response jobId we fetch the blockchain job started previously. Request Body Name Type Description jobId * String (use previously returned jobId, as for example 59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e) Copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your own API Key). curl Javascript Phyton You should receive a response indicating the completed job and the related blockchain receipt and certificate. Fetch API Call Response Congratulations! In just one call, you used BWS to save data into the Web3 blockchain ecosystem. Previous Welcome! Next Platform Fees Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Get your API key Your First BWS API Request Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. Check for Status Fetch for the blockchain job status and results. Was this helpful? Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }' Copy var settings = { "url": "https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call", "method": "POST", "timeout": 0, "headers": { "X-Api-Key": "API-KEY", "Content-Type": "application/json" }, "data": JSON.stringify({ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }), }; $.ajax(settings).done(function (response) { console.log(response); }); Copy import http.client import json conn = http.client.HTTPSConnection("api.bws.ninja") payload = json.dumps({ "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }) headers = { 'X-Api-Key': 'API-KEY', 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } conn.request("POST", "/v1/call", payload, headers) res = conn.getresponse() data = res.read() print(data.decode("utf-8")) Copy { "statusCode": 200, "statusMessage": "", "info": { "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" } } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" }' Copy var settings = { "url": "https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch", "method": "POST", "timeout": 0, "headers": { "X-Api-Key": "API-KEY", "Content-Type": "application/json" }, "data": JSON.stringify({ "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4512-8aca-51d06f69f25e" }), }; $.ajax(settings).done(function (response) { console.log(response); }); Copy import http.client import json conn = http.client.HTTPSConnection("api.bws.ninja") payload = json.dumps({ "jobId": "59d0f46b-0b58-4512-8aca-51d06f69f25e" }) headers = { 'X-Api-Key': 'API-KEY', 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } conn.request("POST", "/v1/fetch", payload, headers) res = conn.getresponse() data = res.read() print(data.decode("utf-8")) Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "status": { "Value": "completed" }, "request": { "solution": "BWS.Blockchain.Hash", "version": 1, "network": "matchain", "operation": "insert", "parameters": { "key": "timestamp: 1736418591440", "value": "Hola" } }, "hash": "0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8", "txnUrl": "https://matchscan.io/tx/0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8", "certificateOfTrust": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/bws-backoffice-website-infra-prod/tx/matchain/0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8.pdf", "certificateOfTrustJPG": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/bws-backoffice-website-infra-prod/tx/matchain/0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8.jpg", "certificateOfTrustUrl": "https://prod.bws.ninja/tx.html?t=0xd5c51886e57b1b16e59bd866910f2c689728f9e4025a4abc14bf7b07ae21dcc8", "type": { "value": "blockchain-job" }, "timestampInMillis": 1736418605359, "id": "a89bf8bf-392b-4cc3-8ded-7dbe9d43c341" } }

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash Solution Overview Operations BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.Blockchain.Hash Solution Overview Use the blockchain to efficiently store data in blockchain(s). BWS.Blockchain.Hash simplifies saving data to the blockchain, enabling you to use it as a hash database while abstracting the complexities of blockchain infrastructure. What is a Hash Database? A hash database is a type of database that uses hash keys to efficiently retrieve and store data. Each record is assigned a unique key, often derived from the data or predefined attributes, which acts as an identifier for quick lookups. Hash databases are known for their speed and scalability, making them ideal for applications requiring rapid data access. An example: saving client data to the blockchain. Businesses can use BWS.Blockchain.Hash to manage client data by storing essential information (e.g., customer profiles or transaction records) in a blockchain-based hash database. The system uses unique keys, such as client IDs or transaction numbers, to retrieve data effortlessly. Here's how this could work: Client Information Use a unique client ID as the hash key to link to stored client details or references. Data Retrieval Quickly access data associated with a hash key for reporting, auditing, or verification purposes. Enhanced Trust Leverage blockchain’s immutable nature to ensure the integrity and transparency of stored records. Why Choose BWS.Blockchain.Hash? This solution is perfect for individuals and businesses that want to take advantage of blockchain as a trusted and efficient hash-based storage system. With BWS.Blockchain.Hash , you can store and retrieve data securely, gain the benefits of blockchain transparency, and provide clients with a Certificate of Trust for each entry—ensuring their data is handled responsibly and verifiably. Available Networks We're currently updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon (main) and Mumbai (test). Please use Matchain. BWS.Blockchain.Save streamlines the process of storing data on the blockchain by abstracting away the complexities of blockchain infrastructure. With this solution, individuals and enterprises can effortlessly leverage the security and trustworthiness of blockchain technology without facing steep learning curves or high costs. Easily write data to the blockchain and receive a Certificate of Trust , providing verifiable proof of the data's integrity and authenticity. BWS.Blockchain.Save is the perfect tool for those who want the benefits of blockchain without the technical overhead. Upload to IPFS Use this operation to upload content to IPFS and get the IPFS Content Identifier (CID). POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the IPFS CID and useful URIs. Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "info" : { "cid" : "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "uris" : { "ipfs" : "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "url" : "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } Upload to IPFS Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption description string Short description of your content (will be returned when listing your IPFS files ). content JSON / base64 Content to save on the IPFS network. We currently support: JSON (just pass the JSON or the stringified string) File sent as a base64 encoded string Please note that when uploading your file: You must optimize your content to be less than 1 MB in size (there is a hard limit of 5MB when uploading files to IPFS using our API). We currently support JSON, images, and PDF files. If your use case requires higher file sizes or other file types, please contact us. Upload to IPFS Examples Upload an Image using nodejs Upload JSON using cURL Upload to IPFS call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the IPFS Content Identifier (CID) along with the corresponding IPFS URI and URL. These details facilitate easy access and reference to the uploaded content on the IPFS network. List IPFS Files Get the list of your IPFS files POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string list parameters JSON check Method Parameters 200 The list of files you uploaded to IPFS List IPFS Files Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption from long Unix time in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files from. to long Unix time in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files to. LIST IPFS Files Request Example cURL List IPFS Files call Response Once executed correctly, you will get a list of the IPFS uploaded files using BWS API. That list will contain for each file: the timestamp of creation ( Unix time in milliseconds), the IPFS Content Identifier (CID), the file type, and the URIs to easily access the file IPFS URI, web URL - using the BWS IPFS Gateway to fetch the file. A5k2V729v', /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, data : JSON.stringify(request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console.log(JSON.stringify(response.data)); }) .catch((error) => { console.log(error); }); Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: XqaLg...729v' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "new", "parameters": { "description": "Just a simple JSON for testing BWS API", "content": { "text": "Hello World!" } } }' Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": { "cid": "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } Copy { "statusCode": 200, "statusMessage": "", "info": [ { "": "" } ] } Copy curl --location 'https://api.staging.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: XqaLg...729v' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.IPFS.Upload", "operation": "list" "parameters": { "from": 1700404140000 "to": 1700411940000 } }' Copy { "statusCode": 200, "info": [ { "timestamp": "1700416540067", "cid": "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "description": "Just a simple JSON for testing BWS API", "fileType": "json", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } }, { "timestamp": "1700121600009", "cid": "QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu", "description": "image for token 1325", "fileType": "image", "uris": { "ipfs": "ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu", "url": "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu" } } ] }

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the BWS IPFS Upload service for storing images, PDFs, and JSON files on the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS). The service provides decentralized, content-addressed storage with a dedicated gateway at ipfs.bws.ninja for easy file retrieval using standard HTTP protocols.
useCases: Store NFT metadata and images for blockchain applications
Host decentralized content delivery for static resources like logos and graphics
Share structured data (JSON) in a transparent, verifiable way
Ensure data integrity and provenance for immutable files
Upload and distribute PDF documents across IPFS network
pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. BWS IPFS API Service leverages the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) , a distributed, peer-to-peer storage protocol designed to create a permanent and decentralized method of storing and sharing data. IPFS breaks files into smaller chunks, distributes them across the network, and assigns each file a unique content identifier (CID) for efficient retrieval. BWS IPFS Gateway Use ipfs.bws.ninja as the gateway to access the files you have uploaded to IPFS using BWS. An IPFS gateway acts as a bridge between the decentralized IPFS network and traditional web browsers, allowing users to access content stored on IPFS using standard HTTP protocols. This makes IPFS content easily accessible without requiring specialized software or configuration. Check out our lovely unicorn on IPFS using the BWS Gateway: https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmU7avmnTb4iVbNGCHMJmdEbsx9nRtUx7dWGpKeE7zjo8T Key Features of IPFS Decentralized Storage Files are stored across multiple nodes, enhancing reliability and fault tolerance. Content Addressing Data is retrieved using its CID, ensuring that files are immutable and tamper-proof. Global Accessibility IPFS data can be accessed by any node in the network, fostering collaboration and resilience. Supported File Types Our service supports uploading images. PDF and JSON files , making it ideal for use cases where these formats are predominant. Examples include: NFT Metadata and Assets Storing JSON metadata files and associated images for blockchain applications. Decentralized Content Delivery Hosting static resources like logos or graphics for web or app projects. Data Integrity and Provenance Sharing structured data (e.g., JSON) in a transparent, verifiable way. Previous BWS.IPFS.Upload Next Operations Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? BWS IPFS Gateway Key Features of IPFS Supported File Types Was this helpful?
